verses.latin_vulgate: Senior Gajo carissimo, quem ego diligo in veritate.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ πρεσβύτερος γαΐῳ τῷ ἀγαπητῷ, ὃν ἐγὼ ἀγαπῶ ἐν ἀληθείᾳ
verses.king_james: The elder unto the wellbeloved Gaius, whom I love in the truth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Carissime, de omnibus orationem facio prospere te ingredi, et valere sicut prospere agit anima tua.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀγαπητὲ, περὶ πάντων εὔχομαί σε εὐοδοῦσθαι καὶ ὑγιαίνειν, καθὼς εὐοδοῦταί σου ἡ ψυχή
verses.king_james: Beloved, I wish above all things that thou mayest prosper and be in health, even as thy soul prospereth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Gavisus sum valde venientibus fratribus, et testimonium perhibentibus veritati tuæ, sicut tu in veritate ambulas.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐχάρην γὰρ λίαν ἐρχομένων ἀδελφῶν, καὶ μαρτυρούντων σου τῇ ἀληθείᾳ, καθὼς σὺ ἐν ἀληθείᾳ περιπατεῖς
verses.king_james: For I rejoiced greatly, when the brethren came and testified of the truth that is in thee, even as thou walkest in the truth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Majorem horum non habeo gratiam, quam ut audiam filios meos in veritate ambulare.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μειζοτέραν τούτων οὐκ ἔχω χαρὰν, ἵνα ἀκούω τὰ ἐμὰ τέκνα ἐν ἀληθείᾳ περιπατοῦντα
verses.king_james: I have no greater joy than to hear that my children walk in truth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Carissime, fideliter facis quidquid operaris in fratres, et hoc in peregrinos,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀγαπητὲ, πιστὸν ποιεῖς ὃ ἐὰν ἐργάσῃ εἰς τοὺς ἀδελφοὺς καὶ εἰς τοὺς ξένους
verses.king_james: Beloved, thou doest faithfully whatsoever thou doest to the brethren, and to strangers;

verses.latin_vulgate: qui testimonium reddiderunt caritati tuæ in conspectu ecclesiæ: quos, benefaciens, deduces digne Deo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἳ ἐμαρτύρησάν σου τῇ ἀγάπῃ ἐνώπιον ἐκκλησίας οὓς καλῶς ποιήσεις προπέμψας ἀξίως τοῦ θεοῦ
verses.king_james: Which have borne witness of thy charity before the church: whom if thou bring forward on their journey after a godly sort, thou shalt do well:

verses.latin_vulgate: Pro nomine enim ejus profecti sunt, nihil accipientes a gentibus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὑπὲρ γὰρ τοῦ ὀνόματος ἐξῆλθον, μηδὲν λαμβάνοντες ἀπὸ τῶν ἐθνῶν
verses.king_james: Because that for his name's sake they went forth, taking nothing of the Gentiles.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nos ergo debemus suscipere hujusmodi, ut cooperatores simus veritatis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἡμεῖς οὖν ὀφείλομεν ἀπολαμβάνειν τοὺς τοιούτους, ἵνα συνεργοὶ γινώμεθα τῇ ἀληθείᾳ
verses.king_james: We therefore ought to receive such, that we might be fellowhelpers to the truth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Scripsissem forsitan ecclesiæ: sed is qui amat primatum genere in eis, Diotrephes, non recipit nos:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἔγραψα τῇ ἐκκλησίᾳ ἀλλ᾽ ὁ φιλοπρωτεύων αὐτῶν διοτρεφὴς οὐκ ἐπιδέχεται ἡμᾶς
verses.king_james: I wrote unto the church: but Diotrephes, who loveth to have the preeminence among them, receiveth us not.

verses.latin_vulgate: propter hoc si venero, commonebo ejus opera, quæ facit, verbis malignis garriens in nos: et quasi non ei ista sufficiant, neque ipse suscipit fratres: et eos qui suscipiunt, prohibet, et de ecclesia ejicit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: διὰ τοῦτο ἐὰν ἔλθω, ὑπομνήσω αὐτοῦ τὰ ἔργα ἃ ποιεῖ, λόγοις πονηροῖς φλυαρῶν ἡμᾶς καὶ μὴ ἀρκούμενος ἐπὶ τούτοις, οὔτε αὐτὸς ἐπιδέχεται τοὺς ἀδελφοὺς, καὶ τοὺς βουλομένους κωλύει, καὶ ἐκ τῆς ἐκκλησίας ἐκβάλλει
verses.king_james: Wherefore, if I come, I will remember his deeds which he doeth, prating against us with malicious words: and not content therewith, neither doth he himself receive the brethren, and forbiddeth them that would, and casteth *them* out of the church.

verses.latin_vulgate: Carissime, noli imitari malum, sed quod bonum est. Qui benefacit, ex Deo est: qui malefacit, non vidit Deum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀγαπητὲ, μὴ μιμοῦ τὸ κακὸν, ἀλλὰ τὸ ἀγαθόν ὁ ἀγαθοποιῶν ἐκ τοῦ θεοῦ ἐστιν ὁ δὲ κακοποιῶν οὐχ ἑώρακεν τὸν θεόν
verses.king_james: Beloved, follow not that which is evil, but that which is good. He that doeth good is of God: but he that doeth evil hath not seen God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Demetrio testimonium redditur ab omnibus, et ab ipsa veritate, sed et nos testimonium perhibemus: et nosti quoniam testimonium nostrum verum est.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: δημητρίῳ μεμαρτύρηται ὑπὸ πάντων, καὶ ὑπ᾽ αὐτῆς τῆς ἀληθείας καὶ ἡμεῖς δὲ μαρτυροῦμεν, καὶ οἴδατε ὅτι ἡ μαρτυρία ἡμῶν ἀληθής ἐστιν
verses.king_james: Demetrius hath good report of all *men*, and of the truth itself: yea, and we *also* bear record; and ye know that our record is true.

verses.latin_vulgate: Multa habui tibi scribere: sed nolui per atramentum et calamum scribere tibi.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πολλὰ εἶχον γράφειν, ἀλλ᾽ οὐ θέλω διὰ μέλανος καὶ καλάμου σοι γράψαι
verses.king_james: I had many things to write, but I will not with ink and pen write unto thee:

verses.latin_vulgate: Spero autem protinus te videre, et os ad os loquemur. Pax tibi. Salutant te amici. Saluta amicos nominatim.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐλπίζω δὲ εὐθέως ἰδεῖν σε, καὶ στόμα πρὸς στόμα λαλήσομεν Εἰρήνη σοι ἀσπάζονταί σε οἱ φίλοι ἀσπάζου τοὺς φίλους κατ᾽ ὄνομα
verses.king_james: But I trust I shall shortly see thee, and we shall speak face to face. Peace *be* to thee. *Our* friends salute thee. Greet the friends by name.
